subtasks.description: Query SQL to identify top spending publishers with total spend and product category breakdown (counts and spend by product type)

Reconcile publisher spend data with contract terms, commitment history, and competitive alternatives to assess negotiation leverage for each top publisher (market concentration, switching costs, renewal timing, margin impact)

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks, minimum decomposition: (1) sql_gen at mid-tier to aggregate multi-dimensional spend data (total + product mix breakdown requires grouping and window functions), (2) cross_recon at frontier-tier because leverage assessment requires synthesizing spend patterns with contract metadata, renewal windows, and competitive landscape—this is multi-signal reconciliation that demands complex reasoning to produce actionable negotiation recommendations. No separate policy_action needed; leverage classification is part of the cross_recon analysis, not a compliance gate.

output.sql: SELECT publisher, SUM(amount_usd) as total_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT product) as product_count, product, COUNT(*) as product_event_count, SUM(amount_usd) as product_spend FROM consumption_facts GROUP BY publisher, product ORDER BY total_spend DESC, publisher, product
